holdings.why: Profitable from $200 avg. TV: NEUTRAL — not a strong setup but Apple Services and AI features provide durable support. Hold; no catalyst to exit.
TV: BUY, scan STRONG BUY. AI chip interconnect demand (Astera Labs) is a long-term structural play. Profitable from $76 avg — hold and let the trend work.
TV: BUY. AMD basing in a VCP pattern — MI300X AI GPU series has meaningful market share vs Nvidia. If underwater from $265, the recovery thesis is intact; hold with patience.
TV: BUY. Applied Digital is an AI data center infrastructure play riding the same AI buildout tailwind as IRM and DLR. Hold the thesis.

TV: SELL, scan: WEAK — near cost basis $423. The setup has deteriorated; do not add. Hold the position with a mental stop below $400, and reduce if the stock breaks that level or TV downgrades to STRONG SELL.
Thin data (no fundamentals available in scan), no TV rating. Hold the speculative position — unclear near-term direction.

TV: SELL — ad revenue competition from AI assistants is a real structural risk. Near cost basis of $215 avg. Hold but monitor closely; YouTube and Google Cloud remain strong counterarguments to the AI disruption thesis.

TV: BUY, financial sector leading the market. Goldman is a clear beneficiary of M&A revival, rising interest income, and financial sector rotation. Profitable from $670 avg — hold and let the financial uptrend carry this.
TV: BUY. Robinhood has become a real fintech platform — crypto and options trading drive revenue. Slightly profitable from $91.17 avg. Hold.

TV: BUY. Intel turnaround under new CEO is the thesis — foundry business (Intel 18A process) and AI PC chips. Profitable from $45 avg if Intel has recovered. Hold with patience; this is a 12-18 month recovery trade.
Single-share speculative position in crypto mining. Hold — no material impact on portfolio regardless of outcome.
Lam Research — semiconductor equipment leader. Massive winner from $289 avg. The semiconductor equipment upcycle driven by AI-related fab investment is multi-year. Hold.
Intuitive Machines, scan: WEAK. Space exploration is speculative and at near breakeven from $20 avg. Hold the small position — SpaceX SPCX listing has reinvigorated the space sector narrative.
Profitable from $345 avg. Azure AI cloud revenue growing strongly. TV not SELL (unlike GOOGL/AMZN). Hold for continued compounding — Copilot monetization is the long-term catalyst.
Micron — memory cycle has turned. HBM (high-bandwidth memory) for AI training is driving pricing. Profitable from $245 avg. Hold.
Nvidia — the AI infrastructure leader. Massively profitable from $165 avg. Hold; Blackwell GPU demand is insatiable and the long-term AI buildout has years to run.
Oracle — massive winner from $100 avg. OCI cloud growth plus AI database demand (co-location of AI workloads on Oracle's network) has driven a fundamental re-rating. Hold.
Palantir, scan: WEAK. Profitable from $120 avg. Government AI contracts are a durable moat. WEAK setup means it's basing after extended run — hold but don't add until the setup improves.
D-Wave Quantum, speculative. 1000 shares at $4.66 avg is small dollar risk. Quantum computing is a 5-10 year thesis. Hold the speculative position.
2x leveraged QQQ ETF. From $51 avg, massively profitable as QQQ has been in a multi-year uptrend. Hold — QQQ remains above MA50, tech rotation does not invalidate the index trend.
Rubrik — cybersecurity data protection platform. Profitable from $56 avg. Hold; cybersecurity sector is in an uptrend and Rubrik's ransomware recovery platform addresses a durable enterprise need.
Rigetti Computing, quantum speculative. Profitable from $6 avg. Hold the small speculative position — quantum timeline has extended but the narrative periodically drives the stock.

Rocket Lab, scan: WEAK. 4x gain from $21 avg to ~$84. WEAK setup means the stock needs to consolidate. Hold the large unrealized gain with a mental stop — RKLB is a legitimate SpaceX alternative in small satellite launches.
Super Micro Computer — AI server infrastructure. Massive winner from $23 avg. Accounting concerns from prior year are largely resolved; AI server demand continues. Hold.

SanDisk (NAND flash pure-play post-WDC spinoff). From $450 avg, now ~$1963 = 4.3x gain. Exceptional winner. Hold — NAND flash supply/demand has normalized and AI storage demand supports future growth.

Snowflake — data cloud platform. Hold from $240 avg. Competition from Databricks and BigQuery intensifying, but Snowflake's customer base and data sharing marketplace provide defensibility. Monitor quarterly NRR closely.
2x leveraged SPY ETF. Profitable from $44 avg. SPY is above MA50 in confirmed uptrend. Hold — only exit if market turns to CORRECTION or distribution days accelerate above 5.
Tesla from $439 avg. FSD (Full Self-Driving) and robotaxi monetization are the thesis catalysts. Volatile; hold current position without adding until a clear technical setup forms.
TSMC — TV: BUY. Massively profitable from $175 avg. The global semiconductor foundry with no equal in sub-3nm processes. Hold; AI chip demand means every NVDA/AMD chip requires TSMC's foundry.
Vertiv — AI data center cooling and power. Massive winner from $16 avg. Hold; the AI data center buildout is a multi-year tailwind for thermal management and power distribution.

Apple from $250 avg. TV: NEUTRAL — not a buy trigger but not a sell signal. Services revenue and AI features provide durable support. Hold; no catalyst to exit and the risk of being out of Apple on a product cycle catalyst is real.
Amkor Technology — semiconductor packaging. TV: BUY. Profitable from $83 avg. Semiconductor packaging demand growing with advanced AI chip complexity. Hold.

Vita Coco — profitable from $78.71 avg to $82.74. TV: BUY, scan: WATCH (RSI 77 overbought). Do NOT add at current levels — RSI 77 is the highest overbought reading in the picks list. Hold the existing 3000-share position but wait for RSI to reset to 55-60 before adding more.

Dell Technologies — TV: BUY, scan: STRONG BUY. Profitable from $417.23 avg to $427.78 on 1500 shares. The 6-contraction VCP (2.3% tightest) with RSI 47.8 is a pristine unextended setup. Buy additional shares on any pullback to $415-425. AI server demand (+88% revenue) is multi-year.
Diodes Inc — TV: BUY. Semiconductor analog/mixed-signal. Profitable from $122.81 avg likely. Hold the position in the semiconductor upcycle.

DigitalOcean — TV: SELL, underwater from $179.89 avg to $157.18 (11.9% loss, ~$58K unrealized on 2600 shares). The conflict: our scan rates this STRONG BUY technically but TV aggregates to SELL (6 buy, 10 sell, 10 neutral). The weight of evidence favors reducing this large underwater position. Reduce 30-50% of shares on any bounce; maintain a stop at $153.

Flex Ltd — TV: BUY, scan: STRONG BUY. Essentially flat from $152.31 avg to $151.85. VCP is forming — hold and let the setup develop. Flex is an EMS/contract manufacturer benefiting from supply chain reshoring.

Fortinet — TV: BUY, scan: STRONG BUY (best tech setup in the scan). Profitable from $144.90 avg to $148. RSI at 48.8 means this is completely unextended. Add to this position on any intraday dip to $145-148. EPS +29%, RS at new highs.
Corning — TV: BUY. Slight profit from $189.20 avg to $194.07. Fiber optic demand for AI data center interconnects is a structural tailwind. Hold.

Alphabet — TV: SELL. Very large position (1640 shares) significantly underwater from $363.53 avg. TV SELL signal reflects deteriorating technical momentum. AI search disruption risk is real (Perplexity, ChatGPT search). Reduce 25-30% of shares to limit exposure — this is the largest single risk in this portfolio. Cloud and YouTube remain strong but cannot fully offset the core search headwind.
ICHOR Holdings — TV: BUY. Profitable from $85.66 avg to $92.20. Semiconductor etch gas delivery components — direct semiconductor equipment exposure. Hold.

Eli Lilly — scan: STRONG BUY. Slightly underwater from $1162 avg to ~$1107. GLP-1 drug demand (Zepbound, Mounjaro) is a multi-decade secular growth story. Hold with confidence — the thesis is intact and the slight underwater position is noise against the multi-year growth runway.

Liquidia Corp — profitable from $75.41 avg. Biotech with imatinib for pulmonary arterial hypertension. RSI ~80 (overbought) — hold the position but do not add at current overbought levels. Place a mental stop at $70 to protect gains.
Southwest Airlines from $42.75 avg. Airline travel demand remains solid. Elliott Management activist involvement provides a corporate action catalyst. Hold.

MaxLinear — TV: BUY. Underwater from $99.95 avg to $89.08. Broadband infrastructure semiconductor. The recovery is in progress — hold with patience; do not add until price recovers above the $90-95 area.
NetApp — scan: WATCH. Essentially flat from $159.26 avg to $157.82. Hybrid cloud storage with stable enterprise demand. Hold — no reason to exit but not a high-conviction add either.

Priority Technology Holdings — 30,000 shares, slightly underwater from $7.38 avg to $7.05. Small-cap payments fintech. Hold — the dollar position is meaningful at this size; monitor for any fundamental deterioration.

Smith & Wesson — TV: STRONG BUY, scan: STRONG BUY (score 93.9). Profitable from $16.04 avg to $16.67. 3-contraction VCP (2.6% tightest) at the exact 52-week high. Add to this winner — the setup is among the cleanest in the entire scan and TV unanimously confirms. Best-quality small-cap setup currently available.
Apple from $240 avg. TV: NEUTRAL. Slight profit. Hold — no urgency to exit or add.
Goldman Sachs from $800 avg. TV: BUY. Financial sector is the leading sector in the current rotation. Hold — GS benefits from rising M&A activity and trading volumes in an uptrend market.

Monster Beverage — scan: STRONG BUY, TV: BUY. Excellent winner from $80 avg to $93.69. The 3-contraction VCP at all-time highs means this trend is still early. Hold and let this compounder run — it's one of the top setups in the consumer sector.

SpaceX — NEW LISTING. Slightly underwater from $160.95 avg to $156.11. SpaceX's launch monopoly (Falcon 9), Starlink subscriber growth, and Starship development make this a long-term conviction hold despite the thin trading history. No TV rating yet — expected for a new IPO. Hold; the business thesis has not changed.
Tesla from $250 avg. Profitable if TSLA is above $250. FSD and robotaxi catalyst pipeline intact. Hold for the autonomous vehicle monetization story.
Apple from $259.38 avg. TV: NEUTRAL. Apple Services momentum and Vision Pro/AI devices provide long-term support. Hold — single position portfolio, no urgency to change.
Apple from $250 avg. TV: NEUTRAL. Hold — Services and AI features provide durable support.

Applied Materials — TV: BUY, scan: STRONG BUY. Profitable from $140 avg. Semiconductor equipment leader directly benefiting from AI-driven fab investment. Add on any pullback — AMAT is a core position in the AI infrastructure buildout.

Amazon — TV: SELL, underwater from $250 avg. Small 3-share position limits dollar risk. AWS cloud and Prime remain strong, but TV SELL reflects near-term technical weakness. Hold the small position; do not add until TV rating improves.

Coinbase — TV: STRONG SELL. Up ~98% from $80 avg to ~$158 — an excellent gain on a volatile asset. TV STRONG SELL is a clear signal that momentum has turned bearish. Reduce at least 50% of the position at current levels. Lock in the crypto rally gains before the technical deterioration accelerates.

DTE Energy — TV: STRONG BUY, scan: STRONG BUY. Profitable from $100 avg. Utility with pristine VCP at new highs. AI data center power demand in the Midwest is a structural tailwind. Add to this winner.
EOG Resources — TV: NEUTRAL. Tiny 1-share position, massive gain from $11 avg. Hold the single share — no portfolio impact either way.

Fair Isaac Corp — TV: SELL, up 45% from $760 avg to ~$1102 (2 shares). TV SELL flags deteriorating technical momentum on this credit scoring leader. Sell 1 share to lock in ~$342 profit per share and let the remaining share ride with a mental stop at $1000.
Goldman Sachs — TV: BUY. Massive winner from $140 avg. Financial sector is the leading sector in today's market rotation. Hold — this is a core financial sector position working exactly as intended.

Intel — TV: BUY. Recovery thesis under new CEO. From $44.29 avg. Intel 18A process node and AI PC chip (NPU integration) are the key catalysts. Hold with patience — this is a 12-18 month recovery play.
Nvidia — the center of the AI infrastructure universe. Profitable from $280 avg. Blackwell GPU demand is insatiable. Hold; Nvidia's competitive moat (CUDA ecosystem) remains unbreachable near-term.
NXP Semiconductors — TV: BUY. Slight profit from $285.20 avg. Automotive and IoT semiconductor leader. Hold — automotive semiconductor content per vehicle is increasing structurally.

Sirius XM — significantly underwater from $80 avg to ~$28 (65% loss on 10 shares). Dollar loss is manageable (~$520). Sirius faces structural challenges from streaming music but Liberty Media involvement provides event risk potential. Hold the small position; do not add.

SanDisk (NAND flash pure-play) — a generational winner. From $5.45 avg to ~$1963 on 35 shares = ~$68,600 in gains. This is a wealth-defining position. Hold with trailing stop protection — do not sell prematurely, but set a 15-20% trailing stop to protect the extraordinary gains.
Tesla from $400 avg. Hold for FSD monetization and robotaxi catalyst. Volatile — hold the position but consider a stop at $380 to protect against an adverse move.

Western Digital post-SNDK spinoff — HDD-only entity. From $500 avg, 3 shares. Hold this small position — WDC's HDD business benefits from AI data center storage demand (archive tier). Dollar impact is modest.

Bitcoin from $1000 avg — a generational investment. At current Bitcoin prices this is a substantial gain representing years of compounding. Hold; the institutional adoption story (ETF inflows, corporate treasury holdings) continues to provide fundamental support. Do not sell based on short-term price volatility.

Goldman Sachs — TV: BUY, financial sector leading the market. Profitable from $500 avg. Financial sector rotation is in full force with multiple STRONG BUY setups across regional and investment banks. Add to this core financial position — financials are the best-positioned sector in the current uptrend.
